What is claimed is: 1. A compound having the formula ##STR19## wherein: R 1 is H or OH; R 2 is H, CH 3 or OH; R 3 is H, CH 3 , CH 2 CN, CH 2 CH 2 NH 2 or CH 2 CONH 2 ; R I is C 9 -C 21 alkyl, C 9 -C 2...
3. A method for treatment of mycotic infections comprising administering to mammalian subject in need of treatment, an antimycotic amount of a compound of claim 1.
3. A method for treatment of mycotic infections comprising administering to mammalian subject in need of treatment, an antimycotic amount of a compound of claim 1.
3. A method for treatment of mycotic infections comprising administering to mammalian subject in need of treatment, an antimycotic amount of a compound of claim 1.
4. A method for treatment of pneumocystis pneumonia in immune-compromised patients comprising administering a therapeutically effective amount of a compound of claim 1.
4. A method for treatment of pneumocystis pneumonia in immune-compromised patients comprising administering a therapeutically effective amount of a compound of claim 1.
4. A method for treatment of pneumocystis pneumonia in immune-compromised patients comprising administering a therapeutically effective amount of a compound of claim 1.

use_codes.desc: CANCIDAS IS INDICATED FOR EMPIRICAL THERAPY FOR PRESUMED FUNGAL INFECTIONS IN FEBRILE, NEUTROPENIC PATIENTS.

indications.text:
1.1 Empirical Therapy for Presumed Fungal Infections in Febrile, Neutropenic Patients
CANCIDAS® is indicated as empirical therapy for presumed fungal infections in febrile, neutropenic
adult and pediatric patients (3 months of age and older) [see Clinical Studies (14.1, 14.5)].
1.2 Treatment of Cand...

1.1 Empirical Therapy for Presumed Fungal Infections in Febrile, Neutropenic Patients
CANCIDAS® is indicated as empirical therapy for presumed fungal infections in febrile, neutropenic
adult and pediatric patients (3 months of age and older) [see Clinical Studies (14.1, 14.5)].
1.2 Treatment of Cand...

CANCIDAS is indicated for the treatment of:
• Candidemia and the following Candida infections: intra-abdominal abscesses, peritonitis and pleural
space infections. CANCIDAS has not been studied in endocarditis, osteomyelitis, and meningitis due
to Candida.
• Esophageal Candidiasis (see CLINICAL STUD...

CANCIDAS® is indicated as empirical therapy for presumed fungal infections in febrile, neutropenic
adult and pediatric patients (3 months of age and older) [see Clinical Studies (14.1, 14.5)].

• Empirical therapy for presumed fungal infections in febrile, neutropenic patients
• Treatment of candidemia and the following Candida infections: intra-abdominal abscesses, peritonitis
and pleural space infections. C...

CANCIDAS is indicated for the treatment of:
· Esophageal Candidiasis (see CLINICAL STUDIES).
· Invasive Aspergillosis in patients who are refractory to or intolerant of other therapies (i.e.,
amphotericin B, lipid formulations of amphotericin B, and/or itraconazole). CANCIDAS has not been
